key_insight: DT-SegNet combines object detection and transformer-based segmentation to accurately measure precipitate area and size distribution in chromium-based alloy micrographs.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more reliable microstructure quantification for designing high-temperature alloys used in extreme-environment applications such as concentrated solar power.
method: YOLOv5 detects precipitate regions in electron microscopy images.
SegFormer refines the detected regions with high-accuracy segmentation.
The two-stage pipeline is designed for efficient, end-to-end precipitate measurement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DT-SegNet outperforms Weka and ilastik across accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.
watch_item: Performance may depend on image quality and careful training data coverage, so cross-material generalization should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Accurate identification and measurement of the precipitate area by two-stage deep neural networks in novel chromium-based alloys
